species: N2O-reducing enrichment culture (activated-sludge-derived mixed community; nosZ clade I / Pseudomonadaceae-dominated at high D)

growth_records.strain: Chemostat enrichment (inocula: Harnaschpolder and Dokhaven WWTP activated sludge)
growth_records.medium: Mineral medium with acetate as e-donor and N2O as sole e-acceptor (Period I, N2O-limited)

up.method: calculated: qAc = |CH3COO- conversion rate| / total biomass. Table2 acetate rate=6.90 mmol/h; biomass(Cmol)=biomass production rate/D=4.49/0.086=52.2 mmol-Cmol=0.0522 Cmol; gDW=0.0522*24.6 g/Cmol(CH1.8O0.5N0.2)=1.284 g; 6.90/1.284=5.37
calculated: 17.21 mmol/h / 1.284 gDW = 13.40 (Table2 N2O rate; biomass as above)
calculated: 1.24 mmol/h / 1.284 gDW = 0.97 (N source uptake, Table2)

growth_records.cite: Conthe et al. Life on N2O: deciphering the ecophysiology of N2O respiring bacterial communities in a continuous culture. The ISME Journal (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41396-018-0063-7. [PMC5864245]
growth_records.snippet: Period I | N2O | D=0.086 | CH3COO- -6.90 | N2O -17.21 | N2 16.72 | NH4+ -1.24 | biomass 4.49 | CO2 7.57 (Table 2). YXAc 0.33 CmolX/CmolS, YXN2O 0.26 CmolX/molN2O (Table 3).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat: mu=D=0.086 h-1; td=ln2/0.086=8.06 h. Specific rates = Table2 absolute conversion rate (mmol/h) / total reactor biomass. Total biomass(Cmol)=biomass production rate/D=4.49/0.086=0.0522 Cmol; gDW via assumed CH1.8O0.5N0.2 molar mass 24.6 g/Cmol => 1.284 gDW. Negative Table2 values=consumption. Mixed community, not single species (confidence=medium). C-recovery 91%, e- recovery 95%.
